narrative: All four scanners (L1 architecture, L2 determinism, L3 customization, L4 enhancement) independently land on Excellent with the same root cause: round 6 closed all 13 round-5 findings end-to-end and added one over-delivery (a third `fresh_reason` value, `stale_artifact`) without introducing any structural regression. The seven remaining findings are uniformly polish-grade — one medium contract-tightening (unspecified equality rule for `--scope-of-change=auto`), one closed-set documentation gap on `fresh_reason`, and five low/very-low nits — none of which block execution, violate a stated promise, or affect any of the six audited user archetypes.

opportunities.name: Closed-set `fresh_reason` not mirrored to the headless contract
opportunities.description: The discover script emits three `fresh_reason` values (`no_workspace`, `crashed_no_progress`, `stale_artifact`) but the headless contract documents only the first two and SKILL.md prose names only the first two. The third value is emitted by `discover-planning-artifacts.py:194` and would land in `.decision-log.md` (and in JSON output if extended) without ever appearing in the contract file — the exact closed-set drift the `reason` mirror was designed to prevent. Same author, same skill, two different levels of contract hygiene for two structurally identical closed sets.
opportunities.impact: Lifts the contract from `closed-set in one place, drifty in another` to uniformly closed-set; automators can switch on `fresh_reason` values without reading the script source.
opportunities.action: Add a `## fresh_reason values` table to `references/headless-contract.md` mirroring the existing blocker-reasons table, naming all three values and when each fires. Optionally add a third bullet at `SKILL.md:97-100` for the `stale_artifact` case (or collapse it into `crashed_no_progress` at the script layer if the runtime behaviour is identical).

findings.title: `fresh_reason` closed-set not declared in headless contract; `stale_artifact` undocumented
findings.detail: L3 finding (medium-abuse): the `reason` closed set is rigorously declared, mirrored, and annotated with a dual-write rule. The `fresh_reason` closed set lives only inside the script with no contract-file counterpart, and `stale_artifact` (emitted at `discover-planning-artifacts.py:194`) appears nowhere in human-readable documentation.

findings.title: `fresh_reason: stale_artifact` not surfaced in SKILL.md prose
findings.detail: L1 finding N-2 (low) and L2 finding S2 (low): SKILL.md lines 97-100 enumerate only `no_workspace` and `crashed_no_progress`; the third state is rare but real (primary frontmatter exists but stepsCompleted is empty and never reached stage-1). An LLM reading the prose and seeing only two enumerated values may treat the third as undefined and ad-hoc the behaviour.

opportunities.name: `--scope-of-change=auto` equality rule unspecified
opportunities.description: Both `SKILL.md:206-208` and `references/headless-contract.md:16` say the auto-detect mode `compares arrays — identical → polish, differs → semantic`, but neither file defines what equality means. Set vs ordered? Case-sensitive? Whitespace-stripped? Two runs against the same Update payload could produce different polish/semantic classifications depending on how the LLM happens to compare the arrays — exactly the cross-run nondeterminism the headless contract exists to prevent.
opportunities.impact: Pins the determinism floor on the one Update-mode decision that drives version-history writes; eliminates the only cross-run classification ambiguity in the round-7 surface.
opportunities.action: Add one sentence to both files pinning the comparison rule (recommended: unordered set-membership on stripped, lowercased strings — reordering and whitespace do not count as semantic). Do NOT lift this into a script; both inputs are already in conversation context and the operation is one-shot per Update invocation.

findings.title: Equality semantics for Stage-3 auto-diff not defined
findings.detail: L2 finding S1 (medium): `compare arrays` does not pin set-vs-list, case, or whitespace handling. L4 finding F3 (low) flags the adjacent corner: the procedure is also silent on the case where the prior flush block is malformed or missing — recommended safe default is `treat as semantic` (a missing baseline is not evidence of no-change).

opportunities.name: Polish-grade ergonomics and documentation nits
opportunities.description: Four scanner-surfaced low-severity observations that don't block any user archetype and don't violate any stated promise. Grouped here because each is a one-edit fix and none individually warrants a theme.
opportunities.impact: Removes the residual friction for expert-Yolo callers, narrows the compaction window mid-Stage-1b, hardens two regex contracts with intent-comments, and clarifies one decision-log mirroring obligation.
opportunities.action: Apply each fix in a single polish commit; none gates anything else.

findings.title: Strict 4-of-4 short-circuit mildly hostile to experts omitting one obvious dimension
findings.detail: L4 finding F1 (low): a Yolo-style expert who explicitly states mode/scope/diagram-type but lets sources default to auto-discovered PRDs is routed to full 1e. A one-line acknowledgement (`Inferred from your reply: …, confirm?`) when all four dimensions are explicit would close residual Yolo-friction without compromising the audit trail. Current strict path remains correct fallback.

findings.title: No 1b-reply flush before 1e — narrow compaction window
findings.detail: L4 finding F2 (low): if compaction drops mid-1b before the open-floor reply lands in `.decision-log.md`, the reply is lost and the next session re-asks. The 1b window is short so the risk is narrow; optionally add a brief `1b note` line to the decision log immediately after the reply is parsed.

findings.title: SKILL.md is ~3% over the multi-branch soft target
findings.detail: L1 finding N-1 (low, no action required): 259 lines / 4,690 tokens vs the principles file's ~250-line soft guidance for multi-branch SKILL.md. Well under the ~500-line single-purpose hard ceiling. All 22 lines of round-6 growth pass the `what fails if I delete this?` test. If a future round adds another stage-level surface, the natural carve candidate is the seven-row closed-set reason list at SKILL.md:55-67.

findings.title: `\bprd\b` underscore boundary and date-anchored Session regex lack intent-comments
findings.detail: L2 findings S3 and S4 (very low): `\bprd\b` does not match `prd_legacy/` because `_` is `\w` (no current failure mode — HBC convention is kebab-case and the `index.md` gate filters); the date-anchored Session regex contract (ISO 8601, must zero-pad) is not commented as such. Add one-line comments noting the boundary intent and the `do not loosen without changing the template` rule.

findings.title: `reason` dual-write obligation stated only on the SKILL.md side
findings.detail: L3 finding (low doc nit): SKILL.md:67 says `Add new reasons only by extending this list and the contract file together`; the contract file's parallel line says `Add new reasons only by extending this table`. Adding `AND the brief mirror in SKILL.md — they are intentionally duplicated for compaction survival` to the contract makes the mirroring obligation visible from either side.

strengths.title: Round-6 closed all 13 round-5 findings end-to-end
strengths.detail: L1 audit confirms each of N1/N2/N3, M-1/M-2, N-1/N-3/N-4, N-2, N5, SO1/SO2/SO3, L3 (14 items counting both prior-round nomenclature schemes) is resolved with specific file:line evidence. L4 independently re-walked the same closures from the user-journey lens. No carryovers remain into round 7.

strengths.title: Customization surface has converged across three rounds of new headless flags
strengths.detail: L3: `--scope-of-change`, `--update-flow`, and `--allow-migration-without-as-is` all correctly landed on the headless contract surface, NOT in `[workflow]`. The author has resisted three plausible-but-wrong lifts in a row (mode toggle, language override, scope-of-change). `customize.toml` stays at four well-shaped, fully-commented scalars while the headless contract grows — the right asymmetry.

strengths.title: All eight `Patterns BMad has seen pay off` are present and operational
strengths.detail: L4: open-floor opening, soft-gate elicitation, intent-before-ingestion, capture-don't-interrupt, dual-output, parallel review lenses, three-mode architecture, graceful degradation, plus canonical Decision-Log Workspace. The skill is in the same shape-quality bracket as `bmad-product-brief`.

strengths.title: Closed-set discipline on blocker `reason` values with compaction-survival mirror
strengths.detail: Seven `reason` values declared in both SKILL.md:55-67 and headless-contract.md:65-73, with the dual-write requirement stated in prose at SKILL.md:67. The duplication is acknowledged as compaction-survival (the contract file calls it `a compaction-survival mirror, not a separate source of truth`), not multi-source drift.

strengths.title: Intelligence placement on three new round-6 regex/classifier additions
strengths.detail: L2: `fresh_reason` is a closed-set enum classifier driven by structural inputs (no semantic inference); the date-anchored `^##\s+...\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}` Session regex generalizes from the English word `Session` to ISO 8601 structure (Vietnamese `Phiên` now parses); `\bprd\b` replaces a sloppy substring that would have matched `approved/` or `notes-on-the-prd/`. Each change is paired with a focused new test.

strengths.title: Headless audit trail is automator-grade
strengths.detail: 10-flag input contract, 7-reason closed-set blocker table, JSON return carries `review_lenses_run`, per-decision logging requirement, validator auto-fix gated on per-issue `auto_fixable: true` from the script (not LLM judgement). Honours the principles-file standard: `the JSON return is the smallest set of paths the caller needs; the log carries the reasoning`.

strengths.title: 25 tests covering CLI contract, edge cases, and three new round-6 regressions
strengths.detail: L2: `python scripts/tests/run-tests.py` returns `Ran 25 tests, OK (skipped=1)`. Pattern is consistent (subprocess invocation against the CLI, JSON parse, structural assertion) — no mocking, no fixtures-by-side-channel. New regressions caught at the contract level, not at a function-internal seam.

architecture.assessment: L1: Excellent. All 13 round-5 findings closed with specific file:line evidence; the 22-line SKILL.md growth (237 → 258, now 259 with one trailing line counted) is fully load-bearing — every new sentence anchors a real architectural surface (gate / discriminator / branch / sanity check / closed-set mirror), not procedural padding. No structural regressions, no introduced contradictions.

findings.title: SKILL.md ~3% over the multi-branch soft target
findings.detail: 259 lines / 4,690 tokens vs the ~250-line soft guidance; well under the ~500-line single-purpose hard ceiling. No action required.

findings.title: `fresh_reason: stale_artifact` not surfaced in SKILL.md prose
findings.detail: Script emits three `fresh_reason` values; SKILL.md:97-100 enumerates only the first two.

determinism.assessment: L2: Excellent. Round 6 hit three real seams (audit-trail discrimination, language-coupled regex, sloppy substring match) with focused fixes and matching tests. Intelligence placement, contract clarity, regex hygiene, and test coverage are all where they should be. Stage-3 scope-of-change auto-diff is correctly kept in the prompt — both inputs are already in context — but the equality rule wants pinning.
determinism.token_savings: ~4000-8000 tokens saved per typical invocation vs a no-scripts variant (1500-3000 from discover, 1000-2000 per Mermaid block from validate-mermaid, 500-1500 per PRD shard from check-fr-coverage). Round 6 added no new scripts.

findings.title: `--scope-of-change=auto` equality rule unspecified
findings.detail: Set vs ordered, case, whitespace all undefined. Document the rule (do not lift to a script).

findings.title: `fresh_reason` closed-set not tabulated in headless contract
findings.detail: Mirror the existing blocker-`reason` table treatment; include `stale_artifact`.

findings.title: `\bprd\b` underscore exclusion not commented
findings.detail: Python `\b` is a `\w`/`\W` boundary; `_` is `\w` so `prd_legacy/` would not match. No current failure (HBC is kebab-case + `index.md` gate); add one-line comment.

findings.title: Date-anchored Session regex contract not commented as ISO 8601
findings.detail: Comment as `ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) is the contract; do not loosen without updating the decision-log template`.

customization.assessment: L3: Excellent. `customize.toml` is unchanged since round 5 and remains right-sized — four well-shaped, fully-commented scalars with no boolean toggles. The three new round-6 headless flags (`--scope-of-change`, `--update-flow`, `--allow-migration-without-as-is`) were all correctly kept off the customization surface; the author has now resisted three plausible-but-wrong lifts in a row. One concrete fix worth landing: the `fresh_reason` closed-set is enforced in the script but undeclared in the contract.

findings.title: `fresh_reason` closed-set not declared in headless contract
findings.detail: Medium-abuse: `reason` is rigorously declared and mirrored; `fresh_reason` is not. `stale_artifact` is emitted but documented nowhere in the human-readable contract.

findings.title: `reason` dual-write obligation stated only on the SKILL.md side
findings.detail: Add the symmetric `…AND the brief mirror in SKILL.md — intentionally duplicated for compaction survival` to the contract file.

enhancement.assessment: L4: Excellent. Round 6 closed every round-5 enhancement finding (N1 high, N2/N3 medium, N4/N5 low) and every round-3 carryover (SO1/SO2/SO3, L3), with one over-delivery (`fresh_reason: stale_artifact` as a fourth sub-case). All six user archetypes pass; all eight `Patterns BMad has seen pay off` are present and operational. Three polish-grade findings remain — none blocks any archetype or violates a stated promise.

journeys.summary: Activates with partial PRD knowledge; Stage 1a runs the discover script silently and surfaces `no_workspace`. Stage 1b opens with the broad invitation plus wrong-skill off-ramp. Partial reply correctly routes to 1e for confirmation. Zero-actor branch protects data-pipeline domains. No visible friction.
bright_spots: Wrong-skill off-ramp at 1b fires before any heavier work
Zero-actor branch promotes triggers to first-class actors for data-pipeline products

journeys.summary: Wants Yolo. Open-floor reply naming all four dimensions (mode/scope/sources/diagram-type) short-circuits past 1e. The strict 4-of-4 gate is safety-correct but mildly hostile if one obvious dimension is omitted (e.g. sources defaulted to auto-discovered).
friction_points: Strict short-circuit falls through to full 1e when one obvious dimension is implicit (F1)
bright_spots: Audit trail logs which dimensions were inferred from 1b vs confirmed in 1e
Yolo path preserved when all four are explicit

journeys.summary: Activates thinking this is a class-diagram skill (`tạo sơ đồ class cho User module`). Stage 1a runs deterministically with no token spend; Stage 1b's wrong-skill off-ramp fires before consuming the source inventory. Redirected to the right skill at the cheapest possible point.
bright_spots: Wrong-skill detection costs one prompt, no tokens-wasted-on-elicitation

journeys.summary: Zero-actor (data-pipeline) gets the trigger promoted to an actor. Migration-on-greenfield hits the Stage 1e sanity check and either acknowledges via `--allow-migration-without-as-is` or blocks. Targeted single-flow update via U2 / `--update-flow=<name>` re-renders only the named flow. Day-2 re-run resumes via workspace + `fresh_reason` discrimination. Crashed prior run gets `crashed_no_progress`. Localised decision logs parse via date-anchored regex.
bright_spots: Every edge-case has a named branch and a closed-set audit signal
Round-6 over-delivery: `stale_artifact` covers a fourth sub-case (frontmatter exists, no stage-1 yet)

journeys.summary: Customisation resolver missing falls back to hand-merge with `resolver_missing` blocker. Template missing blocks with `template_missing` reason. Compaction mid-Stage-2 survives via the required flush. Compaction mid-Stage-3 is defensible (the primary IS the cache). Compaction mid-Stage-1b has a narrow window where the open-floor reply isn't yet captured (F2).
friction_points: Mid-1b compaction window — open-floor reply not flushed to decision log before 1e (F2)
Stage-3 auto-diff silent on malformed/missing prior flush block (F3)
bright_spots: Stage 2 flush is required-not-optional and survives compaction
All script crashes / missing artefacts route to closed-set blocker reasons with audit

journeys.summary: Headless invocation with 10 flags; closed-set returns. `review_lenses_run` carries which lenses fired. `.decision-log.md` carries all auto-decisions with rationale including `fresh_reason`, scope-of-change classification, and per-issue auto-fix entries citing the validator's `fix_hint`. Meets the principles-file standard to the letter.
friction_points: `fresh_reason: stale_artifact` not in the contract file; automator must read the script to enumerate the closed set
bright_spots: Validator auto-fix gated on per-issue `auto_fixable: true` from the script, not LLM judgement
JSON return is the smallest set of paths the caller needs; the log carries the reasoning

autonomous.notes: 10-flag input contract, 7-reason closed-set blocker table mirrored across SKILL.md and the contract file, JSON return + `.decision-log.md` carry the audit trail. One residual closed-set gap (`fresh_reason` not in the contract) is medium-grade documentation polish, not a blocker.

findings.title: Strict 4-of-4 short-circuit mildly hostile to experts (F1)
findings.detail: Add a one-line `Inferred from your reply: …, confirm?` acknowledgement when 4-of-4 are explicit as a Yolo-friendly midpoint.

findings.title: No 1b-reply flush before 1e — narrow compaction window (F2)
findings.detail: Optional brief `1b note` line to `.decision-log.md` immediately after the open-floor reply is parsed.

findings.title: Stage-3 scope-of-change auto-detect silent on malformed prior flush (F3)
findings.detail: Safe default: `if no prior flush block is parseable, treat as semantic` (missing baseline is not evidence of no-change).

recommendations.action: Add a `fresh_reason values` closed-set table to references/headless-contract.md mirroring the existing blocker-`reason` table, naming all three values (no_workspace, crashed_no_progress, stale_artifact) and when each fires. Optionally add a third bullet at SKILL.md:97-100 for stale_artifact.

Pin the `--scope-of-change=auto` equality rule in both SKILL.md:206 and references/headless-contract.md:16 (recommended: unordered set-membership on stripped, lowercased strings). Add the F3 safe-default sentence: `if no prior flush block is parseable, treat as semantic`.

Polish commit covering the four remaining low/very-low items: F1 one-line Yolo acknowledgement at SKILL.md:130, F2 brief 1b-reply flush, intent-comments on the `\bprd\b` and date-anchored regex in discover-planning-artifacts.py, and the symmetric dual-write note in references/headless-contract.md.
